prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial sequence begins with an archival stock-footage title card. The screen is dark with a centered logo reading “PERISCOPE FILM,” followed by the text “IN DEPTH STOCK FOOTAGE” and “PeriscopeFilm.com.” The image has black borders and a dim, aged-film appearance.

By the next sampled frame, the visuals have changed to a close-up of a record player or toy phonograph mechanism. A tonearm or cartridge labeled “swingster” is positioned above or near a spinning record. The record has a central label, though the writing on it is not clearly readable. A small striped object or part of the mechanism appears near the right side of the turntable. Archival overlay text appears across the lower center, including “PF#: XD30712a” and a timecode, with a PeriscopeFilm website watermark below.

The final sampled frame shows a product package standing upright against a plain light background. The box prominently displays the brand/product name “swingster,” with a smaller word above it that appears to read “Kenner.” The front of the box includes an illustration or photograph of children using or posing with the product, though the details are somewhat blurry. Along the lower front area of the package are smaller images and blocks of text that are not fully legible. Large text near the bottom of the frame reads “BATTERIES AND RECORDS,” though it is partially obscured by the archival timecode overlay. The shot functions visually as a product display or pack shot.
